item.title: Point of Pride is dope as hell!
item.description:
https://bsky.app/profile/taylorlorenz.bsky.social/post/3lrv2i7xsp22b
https://secure.givelively.org/donate/point-of-pride/taylor-lorenz-s-pride-2025-fundraiser-for-point-of-pride Most viral edit: Man you guys ROCK! I can’t thank you enough for upvoting and awarding this post for visibility. At the time of this post, the counter says $17,482, goal is $25k. Thank you so much for the donations!!! There is another post I made today praising Imgur for its kindness and inclusion compared to other sites, I am so proud to see it happen in real time! Original post: When I donated this morning, it was sitting at just under $800. It’s at $4,483 at the time of this post. I’m so glad it’s picking up some traction. I don’t often post about fundraising efforts unless I have donated to them or can verify their fundraising efforts. I’m happy to say in this case I don’t need to check anything except the expiration date on my card I used to donate. Taylor Lorenz is a hero and Point of Pride is a fantastic organization! They are the heroes we need now more than ever to help the LGBTQ community.
Pet tax for fundraiser post. Duke and Charleston (Charlie). Blame their Daycare for that photo.
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Memes and images I stole to show my fiancee, the twenty-fifth part where the sexy villain bounds the main hero and inspired a lot of fetishes.

item.title: Summer shorts [OC]
item.description: So I forgot why I didn’t love wearing shorts in summer, until recently <3 the thigh chair vacuum was enough for me to question my choices, but the puddle of sweat I left behind solidified that I do not care for shorts what so ever! Thankfully it was my own chair :,D

item.title: Acrobatic Artist Bastien Dausse's Incredible Low-Gravity Invention
item.dc:creator: ArcadesCinza

item.title: I'm a professional mocap guy and I just fought the Night King. AMA
item.description: Hey guys, it's been years since I posted one of these behind the scenes but a new one just came out featuring some of my work so I thought I'd share part of it. Links to the trailer and the full length bts down below. I've worked as a mocap stunt actor for nearly two decades now and if you play video games at all you've probably already seen my work. (Battlefield, Indiana Jones, Horizon Forbidden West, Vermintide/Darktide and many more) Shameless edit: I'm also the entire Illuminate faction in Helldivers in case there are any divers here. ?? In addition to playing Jon Snow in this particular trailer for 'Game of thrones: War for Westeros', I was also responsible for the action design and fight choreography so I was quite nervous about how it would be received but I'm pretty happy with the results. Feel free to AMA (although I'm posting this right before bed so please bare with me if I take a while to reply.) Trailer: https://youtu.be/ILU7b29FXFc?si=vvi-feQC8xN6fx-T BTS: https://youtu.be/8bc3aU9nq50?si=KqTklMZVBiI02UgS

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1386 (Foamers)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Major dream last night was about watching 20 minutes of a non-existent film (IRL, anyway) for an Anglo-Saxon lecture series, featuring a medium-budget but rather accurate depiction of the Battle of Hastings. This was followed by a discussion afterward, led by the guy from the British History Podcast. For some reason, the version of the film we were watching was the Polish version, which had the full cut of the film, in the American version, apparently, the film was cut differently and the whole Hastings sequence was removed! The whole film, apparently, was about an autistic bipolar man trying to understand his place in history and his place in a society that seemed hell-bent on crushing himself into dust for the sake of profit. Can't relate to that *at all*. ****sarcasm drips like a leaky faucet**** But not a nightmare. And overall, a good reenactment for having maybe only 500 people at best. But I digress.
Anyone else ever see the film Sunshine? Nice sci-fi thriller.
The one time I was birding in Europe was kind of like that. "Oh, there's a Rock Wren hanging out on this cliff. WAIT. I'm in Greece. What the heck acts like a Rock Wren in Greece?" Turns out they have a *nuthatch* that fills the same niche as rock and canyon wrens in my neck of the woods. That was bizarre.
I feel like a 12th level pile of dust lately.
I'm beginning to think this is the case for anything I interview for and I shouldn't be excited for anything. Well, good news there, I am now starting to not feel *anything*. At all. Other than burnout.
I have a new book recommendation for anyone who likes books, especially if you're familiar at all with classic Arthurian Lore. I just finished The Bright Sword by Lev Grossman yesterday, and it fits very nicely in the classic Thomas Mallory style of Arthurian Legend, where it's a beautiful anachronism stew. It's set after Arthur, and most of his knights are killed by Mordred, and it's about trying to heal a world that no longer exists. Which is very pertinent for all of us right now, I think. I also, unlike The Magicians, wouldn't necessarily call this a subversion of tropes associated with Arthurian lore. Nor is it a modern take on a historical Arthur. If anything, it's more like... a take on the collapse of civilizations through the lens of Arthurian Legend, maybe? But suffice to say, the stars were aligned properly yesterday, and I finished the last 250 pages or so in an afternoon.
No idea if there's an audio book version for it. But yeah, if you like the Arthurian mythos, it's worth reading.
We need a VEI 7 eruption to knock the climate back in sync for a couple years.
This has been me every day. For almost two months. And with no unemployment. I finally got a letter back from the free law service for unemployment. My name was horribly misspelled, and they said they tried calling me (listing a wrong number) and emailing me (wrong email address) and so they dropped my case. I honestly wonder if they would have been any good. I can only assume that I'll never get any unemployment. Welcome to America, where fuck you for your service.
Ah shit, I should have waited to talk about The Bright Sword until this meme. Ah, well. Camelotia is a fragmentary early sauropodomorph.
I sure as heck wouldn't want to run into a Therizinosaurus.
Bushtit, actually.
This has big @PotatoCheesMonk and @BottomShelfVodka vibes.
I'm willing to be paid to make fun of power directly if it comes with a safe pension.
I'm tired of genocides and death being sponsored by my taxes.
Should have saved this for a Trek Dump, oh well.
Lystrosaurus at the end of the Permian
Probably the corvids table.
Phoenix is not walking-friendly.
The good old ancient days.
Interview today in Prescott at 9 AM for the Cellar Lackey position at Superstition. It's my second choice at this point since I haven't heard a word about the teaching job despite thanking them and trying to send a follow-up question. Oh god, what if they read my meme dumps, and that's why they don't want me.
The Eastern Kingbird's scientific binomial name is "Tyrranus tyrranus." Kingbirds in general are known for chasing other birds out of territory.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "Pippin good boy." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 5) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: In case you wonder why they don't shoot and show more IMAX 70mm on actual film stock ...
item.description: That is the 3 hour reel of Oppenheimer - yes, developed and shipped to theaters ... most IMAX theaters show "digital IMAX." And yes, that is a Palm Pilot running the OS that drives the reels ...

item.title: Two Years Ago, Today
item.description: The documentaries they have made about this shitshow are, with full benefit of hindsight, HILARIOUS. Just listening to this arrogant cunt prattle on -- on camera no less -- about how safety protocols are too onerous, makes me cackle out loud every time.

Best girl, may she always catch the squirrels in her perpetual dreams. ,|.. mods for deleting this image.
item.dc:creator: llebkcir

item.title: I wonder how they would feel about Imgur?
item.description: Most Viral edit: Thanks for the upvotes, my dudes. Special shout out to @Lucallia for finding the original source for the comic, the artist is also on Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/smoothdunk2.bsky.social Original post below: For context on this drama, apparently a few people including Mark Cuban returned to Twitter stating that Bluesky was mean to them. I lost the link to the original artist of this comic so if someone has it, you’d be a hero. For additional context, it is true that Bluesky (not unlike Imgur) has a very low tolerance for racism, bigotry, etc, things that seem to be the hallmark of Twitter lately. People are quick to block toxic posters, and the community is great at boosting meaningful content. I’m not turning this into an ad for Bluesky but it is hilarious to see journalists tuck tail and run at the slightest push back. Imgur has its faults and god knows there was time when this place was a cesspool of MAGA and anti trans users. But it has grown over the years to be a more inclusive space. I’m fine with this “echo chamber” that gives some people a safe space to exist online. Our echo chamber says “don’t be a dick to people” and their echo chamber says “Trump is a genius”. My choice is clear.
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars
